verdict_reasoning: EREG is synthesized as a type I single-pass transmembrane precursor (pro-EREG, ~19 kDa glycosylated) that traffics to the basolateral plasma membrane surface before being constitutively cleaved by ADAM17 (and other ADAMs) in the juxtamembrane/ectodomain region, releasing the soluble 46-aa mature ligand as the dominant biological actor. The TM precursor stage is real and surface-accessible (EGF domain faces extracellular space), but it is short-lived and constitutively shed. The soluble form is the canonical signaling molecule. This architecture — small EGF-family proligand with transient TM precursor — maps to contextual/dual_localization: the TM stage qualifies for surface accessibility but is not a stable surface-resident form.
